name: Photovoltaic plant; electric installation; at plant; 3 kWp
description:
This cradle-to-gate unit process dataset is for the production of 1 unit of electric installation for a 3 kWP solar plant.
This unit process includes raw material acquisition; manufacturing; transport; disposal of process waste; ancillary materials manufacturing.

processDocumentation.timeDescription: The period spans from the publication date of the latest study used in Jungbluth et al. (2012) to the publication date of Frischknecht et al. (2020), since the model has remained valid throughout this period.
processDocumentation.geographyDescription: Assumed, since a large fraction of the data used for the inventory in Jungbluth et al. (2012) comes from  Swiss publications.
processDocumentation.technologyDescription:
This process represent the manufacturing of electric installations for photovoltaic systems using average technology for Europe from the calendar years 1991-2006.
It includes all installation between panel and grid, minus the inverter. It contains a fuse box, and cables. These connect panel frames and mounting structure to the lighting arrester, panels to the inverter, and the inverter to the electric meter.
processDocumentation.modelingConstantsDescription: Jungbluth et al. (2012) assumed a standard distance of 60 km is assumed for truck transport,  600 km for train transport of metals, and 200 km for train transport of other materials.
processDocumentation.completenessDescription:
Treatment of Missing Environmental Data
Jungbluth et al. (2012) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered.

Treatment of Missing Technosphere Data
Jungbluth et al. (2012) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered.

Mass Balance
No mass balance was calculated for this process.
processDocumentation.dataSelectionDescription: This unit process was modeled based on Jungbluth et al. (2012) inventory for an electric installation for a  3 kWP plant  available on Table 11.30 of said report.
processDocumentation.dataTreatmentDescription:
The values in the LCI in Jungbluth et al, (2012) reflect the expert judgement of the authors, since they do not seem a weighted average of the different studies.

Process data quality
Based on the information included in the “Data completeness section”, the data quality characteristic (DQC) ""Process completeness"" was given a score of 2 (60-79 % of determined flow have been evaluated and given a value). It is likely the original report went through a review process, but because it is not explicitly mentioned, the data quality characteristic ""Process review"" was left as default (No documented review) and thus with a score of 5.
processDocumentation.samplingDescription:
System boundaries
The system boundary is cradle to gate and includes material use, waste treatment, and transport. 

Data collection
The data that made Jungbluth et al. (2012) LCI is based on previous reports. See the original source for details.

Uncertainty
Jungbluth et al. (2020) included a standard deviation for all flows, which is repeated here.
processDocumentation.dataCollectionDescription: Between calendar years 1991 and 2006, both included, based on the studies cited in Jungbluth et al. (2012)

reviews.details: Colleagues at the Paul Scherrer Institute are acknowledged for their revision of data, but no other details on their review are available.  Because it is not properly documented, the "Process review" data quality entry is left as "5: Not documented".

Colleagues at ESU-services Ldt. are acknowledged for their revision of data, but no other details on their review are available. Because it is not properly documented, the "Process review" data quality entry is left as "5: Not documented".

processDocumentation.intendedApplication:
Situation C2 - Accounting, without system-external interactions - The intended application is a purely descriptive accounting / documentation of the analyzed system excluding interactions with other systems in the LCI model.
The target audience of this model are LCA practitioners, the silicon, solar and related industries and the general public. The inventory is considered largely complete, except for land transformation activities. For that reason, it is not recommended to use this dataset when evaluating land use impact categories. The results of the National Institute of Standards and Technology’s data gap analysis were analyzed using the ISO 21930-LCIA-US impact assessment methodology, as well as Cumulative Energy Demand and a subset of ReCiPe 2016 impact categories.
The original application of this dataset was the identification of data gaps in the Federal LCA Commons (FLCAC) (see ""Project"" above). However, the intended application is to provide a cradle-to-gate inventory (A1-A3, according to ISO 21930) using exclusively processes currently available in USLCI or generated as part of the project mentioned above, and elementary flows from the Federal Elementary Flow List (FEDEFL) to enable the use of a fuller range of LCIA impact categories than those reported in EPDs. For data quality purposes, the intended application is to provide a US average inventory for the year of publication of the original source.
processDocumentation.projectDescription: This dataset was developed as part of the “Public Life Cycle Inventory Data Gap Analysis for Silicon-based Residential Photovoltaic Systems” National Institute of Standards and Technology’s Technical Note.
